title: The Peacock on Our Street
summary: A child in Mumbai tells her grandmother about a peacock that walks down their street every morning.

body.speaker: Anu
body.text: Granny! Come to the window quickly!

body.speaker: Granny
body.text: What is it, Anu? Are you all right?

body.speaker: Anu
body.text: Yes, but look! There is a peacock on our street.

body.speaker: Granny
body.text: Oh, beautiful! He is a big one.

body.speaker: Anu
body.text: His feathers are so blue. And his head is so small.

body.speaker: Granny
body.text: Watch his tail. He may open it now.

body.speaker: Anu
body.text: He is opening it! It is huge! Like a big green fan.

body.speaker: Granny
body.text: Yes, that is his big show. The girl peacocks watch.

body.speaker: Anu
body.text: Where does he live? In the park?

body.speaker: Granny
body.text: Yes, behind the small temple. There are three peacocks in that garden.

body.speaker: Anu
body.text: Why does he come to our street?

body.speaker: Granny
body.text: He looks for food. The man at the corner shop gives him a little rice.

body.speaker: Anu
body.text: Can I give him some bread?

body.speaker: Granny
body.text: Just a little. Stand very still and put the bread on the ground.

body.speaker: Anu
body.text: He is coming closer! Granny, I am a little scared.

body.speaker: Granny
body.text: Do not worry. He is more scared of you than you are of him.

annotations.exampleEn: A peacock has long blue and green feathers.
The bird has soft white feathers.
She opened the paper fan slowly.
The peacock puts on a big show.
There is an old temple behind the school.
I bought milk at the corner shop.
He gives the dog a little rice every day.
Stand very still and the cat will come to you.
My little brother is scared of the dark.

grammarNotes.title: 'There is / there are' to point things out
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi muốn nói có gì ở đâu đó, ta dùng 'There is + danh từ số ít' hoặc 'There are + danh từ số nhiều': 'There is a peacock on our street.' / 'There are three peacocks in that garden.' Đây là cấu trúc rất hay dùng để giới thiệu sự vật mới.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: To say something exists in a place, use 'There is + singular' or 'There are + plural': 'There is a peacock on our street.' / 'There are three peacocks in that garden.' This is the standard way to introduce new things.

grammarNotes.title: Comparative 'more … than' with feelings
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu so sánh 'more X than Y' cho biết bên nào nhiều hơn: 'He is more scared of you than you are of him.' Đây là dạng so sánh đầu tiên người học A1 nên biết, vì nó hay được dùng để trấn an người khác hoặc đối chiếu cảm xúc.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Comparative 'more X than Y' shows which side has more: 'He is more scared of you than you are of him.' This is one of the first comparison patterns A1 learners meet, used a lot to reassure people or compare feelings.

translationVi: Anu: Bà ơi! Bà ra cửa sổ nhanh lên! Bà: Có chuyện gì vậy Anu? Cháu có sao không? Anu: Cháu không sao, nhưng bà nhìn kìa! Có một con công trên đường nhà mình. Bà: Ôi đẹp quá! Một con to thật. Anu: Lông nó xanh quá. Còn đầu thì nhỏ tí. Bà: Cháu nhìn đuôi nó. Có khi nó sắp xòe ra đấy. Anu: Nó đang xòe ra kìa! Lớn quá! Như một cái quạt to màu xanh lá. Bà: Đúng rồi, đó là màn trình diễn lớn của nó. Các cô công xem đấy. Anu: Nó sống ở đâu vậy bà? Trong công viên ạ? Bà: Ừ, ở phía sau ngôi đền nhỏ. Trong vườn đó có ba con công. Anu: Tại sao nó lại đến đường nhà mình hả bà? Bà: Nó đi tìm thức ăn. Bác ở cửa hàng góc đường hay cho nó một ít cơm. Anu: Cháu cho nó miếng bánh mì được không? Bà: Một ít thôi. Cháu đứng thật yên rồi đặt bánh xuống đất. Anu: Nó đang lại gần kìa! Bà ơi, cháu hơi sợ. Bà: Đừng lo. Nó còn sợ cháu hơn cháu sợ nó đấy.

questions.prompt: Why does Anu call her granny?
options: She saw a peacock on the street
questions.explanation: 'There is a peacock on our street.'

questions.prompt: What colour are the peacock's feathers?
What does Granny tell Anu to watch?
questions.explanation: 'Watch his tail. He may open it now.'

questions.prompt: What does the open tail look like?
How many peacocks live in that garden?
questions.explanation: 'There are three peacocks in that garden.'

questions.prompt: Why does the peacock come to their street?
Who gives him a little rice?
options: The man at the corner shop
questions.explanation: 'The man at the corner shop gives him a little rice.'

questions.prompt: What does Anu want to give the peacock?
What does Granny tell Anu to do?
options: Stand very still and put the bread on the ground
questions.explanation: 'Stand very still and put the bread on the ground.'

questions.prompt: How does Anu feel when the peacock comes closer?
What does Granny mean by 'he is more scared of you'?
options: The peacock is afraid of people
questions.explanation: Granny is saying the peacock fears Anu more than Anu fears him.

Granny: Do not worry. He is more scared of you than you are of {{b12}}.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Wild animals sometimes live near people in big cities. What can people do to be kind to these animals?
